test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check non-trivial conditions: test_css_class_consistency verifies CSS selectors match template classes (the base code has a mismatch), test_string_height_preserved evaluates the expression with string values (base code corrupts them with '+px'), test_height_type_accepts_string checks for 'string' in the type definition (base has 'number | "auto"'), test_upload_wrapper_height checks for wrapper height CSS rules (base lacks them). A stub/no-op cannot satisfy these.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available at /workspace/gradio accessible via the solve.sh script content, and the agent could potentially read /solution/solve.sh if it exists in the container (the Dockerfile doesn't explicitly prevent this). Additionally, the test file at tests/test_outputs.py contains explicit expected values and patterns that an agent could grep for.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors from the instruction are tested: CSS class consistency (test_css_class_consistency), string height preservation (test_string_height_preserved), height forwarding (test_height_forwarding_accepts_strings), height type definition (test_height_type_accepts_string), upload wrapper height (test_upload_wrapper_height). informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped into 'fail-to-pass (pr_diff) — core behavioral tests', 'pass-to-pass — regression tests', and 'pass-to-pass (repo_tests) — CI/CD checks'. Each test has a descriptive name and docstring.
